task_completion.description: Create a top-level collection named 'Case Law'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A top-level folder or collection named 'Case Law' is visible in the left pane of the application (e.g., Zotero).

task_completion.description: Create sub-collections 'Federal Courts' and 'State Courts' under 'Case Law'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Folders named 'Federal Courts' and 'State Courts' are visible as sub-collections nested directly under 'Case Law'.

task_completion.description: Create lowest-level collections for 'Supreme Court', 'Courts of Appeals', and 'New York'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Folders named 'Supreme Court' and 'Courts of Appeals' are nested under 'Federal Courts'. A folder named 'New York' is nested under 'State Courts'.

task_completion.description: Sort the Supreme Court cases into the 'Supreme Court' collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Supreme Court' collection is selected, the main pane shows 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', and 'Gideon v. Wainwright', or there is visual evidence of these cases being dragged into the 'Supreme Court' folder.

task_completion.description: Sort the Courts of Appeals case into the 'Courts of Appeals' collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Courts of Appeals' collection is selected, the main pane shows 'United States v. Aluminum Co. of America', or there is visual evidence of this case being dragged into the 'Courts of Appeals' folder.

task_completion.description: Sort the New York case into the 'New York' collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'New York' collection is selected, the main pane shows 'Palsgraf v. Long Island Railroad Co.', or there is visual evidence of this case being dragged into the 'New York' folder.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of the reference manager to create collections and move items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show context menus, drag-and-drop actions, or dialog boxes for creating collections within the application, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual reference management software (e.g., Zotero).
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches a real reference manager, not a text editor or a mocked-up script output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct hierarchy is: 'Case Law' -> 'Federal Courts' -> 'Supreme Court' AND 'Courts of Appeals'; 'Case Law' -> 'State Courts' -> 'New York'. Case assignments: 'Supreme Court' gets Brown v. Board of Education, Miranda v. Arizona, and Gideon v. Wainwright. 'Courts of Appeals' gets United States v. Aluminum Co. of America. 'New York' gets Palsgraf v. Long Island Railroad Co.
